But in C++, the upper_bound returns an iterator. So in code:

#include <vector>

#include <algorithm>

using namespace std;

long long solve(vector<long long> nums, long long k) {

    sort(nums.begin(), nums.end());

    int n = nums.size();

    long long max_len = 1;

    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {

        long long target = nums[i] * k;

        auto it = upper_bound(nums.begin() + i, nums.end(), target);

        int j = it - nums.begin() - 1;

        int len = j - i + 1;

        if (len > max_len) {

            max_len = len;

        }

    }

    return n - max_len;

}
